# Chapter 11 — Information Geometry / Natural Gradient

## Robotics context

Continuous robot policies often output a **distribution** over actions (Gaussian, diffusion, etc.).
Parameters live on a **statistical manifold** — not all coordinates are equally meaningful.

**Natural gradient** descent uses the Fisher information matrix to precondition updates:

```
Δθ = −η F(θ)⁻¹ ∇L
```

This appears in natural policy gradients, TRPO/KL trust regions, and many Physical AI training recipes.

## What this demo shows

1. Expert 2D action demos from an anisotropic Gaussian (different scales per dimension)
2. Fit policy mean μ with **fixed covariance** (known Fisher = precision matrix)
3. Same learning rate: **Euclidean** vs **natural** gradient paths
4. Natural gradient converges smoothly; Euclidean oscillates / stalls

## Failure cases

| Wrong idea | What breaks |
|------------|-------------|
| One global lr on all policy params | Ill-conditioned Fisher → zig-zag or divergence |
| Ignore distribution geometry | Fast change in σ vs slow change in μ |
| Natural gradient without regularization | Fisher near-singular in some regions |
| Treat action samples as Euclidean vectors | Wrong metric for probabilistic policies |

## Run

```bash
python chapters/11_information_geometry/demo.py
```
